task_completion.description: Log into the Aerobridge admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the Django administration dashboard at http://localhost:8000/admin/ after successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Aircraft page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the 'Add aircraft' form page, typically at the URL path /admin/registry/aircraft/add/.

task_completion.description: Enter the required Name and Flight controller ID.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Add aircraft form, the 'Name' field contains exactly 'Phoenix Mk3' and the 'Flight controller id' field contains exactly 'PHXFC2024001'.

task_completion.description: Select Operator, Manufacturer, Final assembly, and Status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Add aircraft form, the 'Operator' and 'Manufacturer' dropdowns have valid selections (not empty/dashes). The 'Final assembly' dropdown has 'Complete' selected, and the 'Status' dropdown has 'Active' selected.

task_completion.description: Save the new aircraft record successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A standard Django admin green success message banner is visible (e.g., 'The aircraft "Phoenix Mk3" was added successfully.') and/or the new aircraft is listed in the Aircrafts table.

task_completion.description: Remain logged into the admin panel after completion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the user is still authenticated in the Django admin interface (e.g., 'Log out' or 'Welcome, admin' is visible in the top right header).

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) navigating the Aerobridge web interface, rather than terminal commands (like curl or python scripts) being used to send HTTP requests.

integrity.description: The success message is generated by the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The success banner should match the standard Django admin UI styling (typically a green bar at the top of the content area) and not appear to be a manually edited HTML page or text file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show the agent using sqlite3, python manage.py shell, or similar backend database commands to insert the aircraft record directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the Aerobridge Django admin interface at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The agent must log in with 'admin' / 'adminpass123'. The target URL for adding an aircraft is http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/add/. The VLM should look for standard Django admin form fields: 'Name' (Phoenix Mk3), 'Flight controller id' (PHXFC2024001), 'Status' (Active), 'Operator', 'Manufacturer', and 'Final assembly' (Complete). Successful creation in Django admin typically displays a green success banner at the top of the list view.
